milestone: Storage Meter — real, origin-wide total (not just World Builder's own footprint)
slice: Direct, pointed follow-up: "but now there cannot be any failed save. also i deleted everything before starting a fresh on this new theme." Confirmed the prior self-heal fix genuinely does NOT make failure impossible, and found the real, previously-missing second cause: World Builder and VihuStudio's own Creator app (Stories, Magic Cards, etc.) are served from the same site, so they share ONE localStorage quota for this browser -- that shared, origin-wide total, not just World Builder's own 3 keys, is what actually decides whether a save here can succeed. A Theme Author who deletes every World Project can still hit a failed save on a brand-new, empty theme if Studio's own separate data is what's filling the shared quota -- and the Browser Storage Meter (built specifically to surface this) only ever measured World Builder's own 3 keys, so it could honestly show 'plenty of room' while the real, whole-origin total was already maxed out. Fixed: ProjectStore.getStorageStats() now also walks every key genuinely present in localStorage (not just the 3 known World-Builder ones) and returns a new originTotalBytes/otherBytes pair alongside the existing totalBytes/byKey; _renderStorageMeter() now bases its percentage bar on the real origin-wide total, and shows an explicit breakdown line ("X is your World Projects here; Y is other VihuStudio data on this site") so the true constraint is finally visible instead of hidden behind a too-narrowly-scoped meter. Verified via a scratch Playwright check seeding a large simulated Studio session blob under the same origin and confirming getStorageStats() correctly attributes it to otherBytes/originTotalBytes rather than hiding it; full regression against both goldenBuild.js suites plus the prior self-heal/signal-dot checks, all unchanged. Disclosed honestly, not oversold: this makes the real constraint visible and gives self-heal a fair shot, but does not make a failed save literally impossible -- an origin already at its ceiling, or a browser with IndexedDB unavailable (private browsing), can still legitimately fail a save. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder architecture change; confined to tools/world-builder-v2/js/projectStore.js, js/worldBuilderApp.js, and css/world-builder.css.
